name: Pitton
overview: Pitton is a historic village about 5-7 miles east of Salisbury in Wiltshire, just off the A30 London Road, forming the joint civil and ecclesiastical parish of Pitton and Farley (Farley lies about 1.5 miles south). Set in a chalk valley, Pitton has the parish church of St Peter (largely rebuilt in 1860, with a fine Kempe stained-glass window), its own primary school, a popular pub, and a shop with Post Office.
character: A pretty, well-served downland village with a strong community, a good village pub and its own school, in a quiet valley east of Salisbury.
amenities: The Hook & Glove pub in neighbouring Farley

schools.name: Secondary/grammar schools in Salisbury (Bishop Wordsworth's, South Wilts)

prices.summary: A sought-after village east of Salisbury; period and chalk-valley cottages plus family homes, with values supported by the school, pub and rural setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: St Peter's Church and its Kempe window
Walks in the chalk valley and to neighbouring Farley
pros: Own primary school, pub and shop/Post Office
Strong community in a pretty chalk-valley setting
Joined with Farley for a wider community
cons: No railway station; rely on Salisbury
whoItSuits: Families wanting a well-served, characterful village east of Salisbury with its own school and a good pub.
